title: Rich Silky Greek Semolina Galaktoboureko
description: A traditional Greek dessert featuring semolina custard in a crispy phyllo pastry, perfect for a sweet treat or hangover cure. This version uses strong wholemeal flour and is FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, whisk together the semolina, milk, and sugar, then cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the melted butter and vanilla extract.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and wholemeal flour, then gradually add the warm semolina mixture, whisking until smooth.
Unroll the phyllo pastry and cut it into 4 equal pieces.
Place a piece of phyllo pastry on a flat surface and brush it with a little bit of melted butter.
Spoon the semolina custard onto the center of the phyllo pastry, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the phyllo pastry over the custard to form a triangle or square shape, and brush the top with a little bit of melted butter.
Repeat the process with the remaining phyllo pastry and custard.
Place the galaktoboureko on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 35 minutes, or until golden brown.
